title: Payment Processing Success Rate and Revenue Leakage
dataSources: `index=business` `sourcetype="payment_gateway"` (transaction_id, amount, currency, payment_method, status, decline_reason, country)
spl:
index=business sourcetype="payment_gateway" earliest=-24h
| eval success=if(status="approved", 1, 0)
| eval declined=if(status="declined", 1, 0)
| eval errored=if(status="error", 1, 0)
| stats sum(success) as approved, sum(declined) as declined, sum(errored) as errors,
        sum(eval(if(success=1,amount,0))) as approved_revenue,
        sum(eval(if(declined=1,amount,0))) as declined_revenue,
        count as total by payment_method
| eval success_rate=round(100*approved/total, 2)
| eval revenue_lost=declined_revenue
| sort - revenue_lost
| table payment_method, total, approved, declined, errors, success_rate, approved_revenue, revenue_lost
description: Monitors payment gateway success, decline, and error rates in real time — every declined payment is potentially lost revenue. Business teams see which payment methods, card types, and regions have the highest failure rates, while engineering teams get immediate alerts on gateway outages. A 1% improvement in payment success rate directly increases revenue.
value: Monitors payment gateway success, decline, and error rates in real time — every declined payment is potentially lost revenue. Business teams see which payment methods, card types, and regions have the highest failure rates, while engineering teams get immediate alerts on gateway outages. A 1% improvement in payment success rate directly increases revenue.
implementation: (1) Forward payment gateway events to Splunk via HEC; (2) include transaction details, decline codes, and amounts; (3) alert immediately when success rate drops below 95% — likely a gateway issue; (4) analyse decline reasons to identify recoverable declines (e.g., retry logic, alternative payment methods); (5) track by country for regional payment method optimisation.
visualization: Single value (overall success rate, revenue lost today), Line chart (success rate over time), Bar chart (decline reasons), Table (performance by payment method).
knownFalsePositives: Spikes when a bank or card network has an incident, new fraud rules go live, or a country rollout changes default payment methods.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HEC (payment gateway logs).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=business` `sourcetype="payment_gateway"` (transaction_id, amount, currency, payment_method, status, decline_reason, country).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Forward payment gateway events to Splunk via HEC; (2) include transaction details, decline codes, and amounts; (3) alert immediately when success rate drops below 95% — likely a gateway issue; (4) analyse decline reasons to identify recoverable declines (e.g., retry logic, alternative payment methods); (5) track by country for regional payment method optimisation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=business sourcetype="payment_gateway" earliest=-24h
| eval success=if(status="approved", 1, 0)
| eval declined=if(status="declined", 1, 0)
| eval errored=if(status="error", 1, 0)
| stats sum(success) as approved, sum(declined) as declined, sum(errored) as errors,
        sum(eval(if(success=1,amount,0))) as approved_revenue,
        sum(eval(if(declined=1,amount,0))) as declined_revenue,
        count as total by payment_method
| eval success_rate=round(100*approved/total, 2)
| eval revenue_lost=declined_revenue
| sort - revenue_lost
| table payment_method, total, approved, declined, errors, success_rate, approved_revenue, revenue_lost
```

Understanding this SPL

**Payment Processing Success Rate and Revenue Leakage** — Monitors payment gateway success, decline, and error rates in real time — every declined payment is potentially lost revenue. Business teams see which payment methods, card types, and regions have the highest failure rates, while engineering teams get immediate alerts on gateway outages. A 1% improvement in payment success rate directly increases revenue.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=business` `sourcetype="payment_gateway"` (transaction_id, amount, currency, payment_method, status, decline_reason, country). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HEC (payment gateway logs). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: business; **sourcetype**: payment_gateway. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=business, sourcetype="payment_gateway", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **success** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **declined** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **errored** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by payment_method** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **success_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **revenue_lost** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Payment Processing Success Rate and Revenue Leakage**): table payment_method, total, approved, declined, errors, success_rate, approved_revenue, revenue_lost


Step 3 — Validate
Compare payment success and failure rates to the payment gateway, merchant, and subscription billing system for the same methods and region.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (overall success rate, revenue lost today), Line chart (success rate over time), Bar chart (decline reasons), Table (performance by payment method).
grandmaExplanation: We watch payment success so we find revenue leaks from declined cards and fix them before they stack up.
